---
title: "TAM/SAM/SOM: Senior Mobility Platform"
source: market-sizing
filed: 2026-03-16
section: market-analysis
---

## Total Addressable Market (TAM)

The global elderly transportation market is valued at $42 billion (2025), growing at 8.3% CAGR. This includes paratransit services, medical transport, assisted ride-sharing, and specialized mobility solutions for the 65+ demographic across developed markets.

## Serviceable Addressable Market (SAM)

Focusing on urban areas in the United States, Canada, and Western Europe where smartphone-assisted booking is viable and regulatory frameworks support autonomous or semi-autonomous vehicles, the SAM narrows to $12.8 billion. This represents cities with populations above 500,000 and established healthcare transport corridors.

## Serviceable Obtainable Market (SOM)

Year 1-3 target: $180 million across 5 pilot cities (Boston, Toronto, Amsterdam, Stockholm, Singapore). These cities share strong public-private healthcare partnerships, existing paratransit infrastructure for integration, and regulatory willingness to pilot autonomous mobility solutions. Initial penetration targets 2.8% of the SAM in pilot geographies.

## Key Assumptions

- Average trip value: $28 (premium over standard ride-share due to assisted boarding, wait time, and door-through-door service)
- Trip frequency: 4.2 trips per user per week (medical + social + errands)
- User acquisition cost: $85 (healthcare partnership channel reduces from industry average $145)
